title: A Quadratic Forcing Observable for Frankl's k = 5 Residual
subtitle: Continuation of the union-closed work: the harness, seeded with the prior topic's ledger, finds nonlinear signal where the no-go theorem said no linear proof could.
summary: The earlier topic ([frankl-union-closed](/research/math/frankl-union-closed)) ended with a no-go theorem: every $S_5$-equivariant linear transfer from edge masses to vertex loads collapses to degree-plus-total-mass, so no symmetric linear scheme can close $\max p_x \geq 0.39$ on the $k=5$ residual. This follow-up scenario, run via the master orchestrator with the prior ledger inherited verbatim, attacked the gap with the linear obstruction baked in as a constraint. The cross-domain pass produced a quadratic observable $Q_x = \sum_{i<j} a_i a_j$ on the local $x$-channel masses that is strictly positive at the LP adversary $b_e = B/10$ where every linear observable vanishes. The accompanying transport inequality reduces Frankl on the residual to a single inequality on an averaged augmentation-success mark.

models.label: Codex GPT-5.4
